repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
<!--
  Thank you for contributing to Neovim!
  If this is your first time, check out https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests-prs
  for our PR guidelines.
-->

## Problem

Current vim.net.request call does not have the ability to add custom headers that can be used for more complex request scenarios like authentication and more.

## Solution

We are instroducing a new `headers` table in vim.net.request `opts` table that will be used to add custom headers in the GET request

comment_data.body:
all validation can live in one test

```suggestion
  it('validation', function()
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -163,4 +163,48 @@ describe('vim.net.request', function()
     t.eq(false, rv.modified)
     t.eq(true, rv.zipfile)
   end)
+
+  it('accepts custom headers', function()
+    t.skip(skip_integ, 'NVIM_TEST_INTEG not set: skipping network integration test')
+
+    local headers = exec_lua([[
+      local done = false
+      local result
+
+      vim.net.request("https://httpbingo.org/headers", {
+        headers = {
+          Authorization = "Bearer test-token",
+          ['X-Custom-Header'] = "custom-value",
+        },
+      }, function(err, res)
+        if err then
+          result = { error = err }
+        else
+          result = vim.json.decode(res.body).headers
+        end
+        done = true
+      end)
+
+      vim.wait(2000, function() return done end)
+      return result
+    ]])
+
+    assert.is_table(headers)
+    -- httpbingo.org/request returns each header as a list in the returned value
+    t.eq(headers.Authorization[1], 'Bearer test-token', 'Expected Authorization header')
+    t.eq(headers['X-Custom-Header'][1], 'custom-value', 'Expected X-Custom-Header')
+  end)
+
+  it('rejects non-table headers param', function()

comment_data.body: there are probably some more specific details we should mention here (depends on curl behavior). some ideas are at https://requests.readthedocs.io/en/latest/user/quickstart/#custom-headers . always worth checking an existing thing like that for comparison.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -4232,6 +4223,8 @@ vim.net.request({url}, {opts}, {on_response})              *vim.net.request()*
                          body to.
                        • {outbuf}? (`integer`) Buffer to save the response
                          body to.
+                       • {headers}? (`table<string, string>`) Table of custom
+                         headers to send with the request.

comment_data.body:
```suggestion
        -- Add headers to the request
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -4220,6 +4206,11 @@ vim.net.request({url}, {opts}, {on_response})              *vim.net.request()*
           outbuf = 0,
         })
         job:close()
+
+        Add custom headers in the request

comment_data.body:
We might want to do some input validation here. A quick scan of `curl(1)` gives us the following concerns:
- Any keys that start with `@` will be treated as filenames by curl.
- Some control characters may be problematic.
- And ofc, a `:` (or a `;`, it seems) as either the key or value (perhaps only a problem for the key?).

Not sure if we want to actually do any validation or just mention this in the docs.

Additionally, `curl` has a special meaning for an empty `value` which we will want to override by using a semicolon when an empty value is provided (see manual page for details). This we will definitely want to account for, regardless of what we do for input validation. The code would be:
```lua
if value == '' then
  vim.list_extend(args, { '-H', key .. ';' })
else
  vim.list_extend(args, { '-H', key .. ': ' .. value })
end
```

Sorry for the nitpicking but it is a network API and we don't want this to do anything surprising.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -76,6 +84,13 @@ function M.request(url, opts, on_response)
     vim.list_extend(args, { '--output', opts.outpath })
   end
 
+  if opts.headers then
+    vim.validate('opts.headers', opts.headers, 'table', true)
+    for key, value in pairs(opts.headers) do
+      vim.list_extend(args, { '-H', key .. ': ' .. value })

comment_data.body:
was hoping not to leak curl specifics into the interface, but i suppose that's inevitable.

```suggestion
---Supports basic key/value header formats as supported by curl.
---Does not support @filename style, internal header deletion (e.g: 'Header:')
---and empty headers 'Header;'.
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -14,6 +14,12 @@ local M = {}
 ---
 ---Buffer to save the response body to.
 ---@field outbuf? integer
+---
+---Table of custom headers to send with the request.
+---Supports basic key/value header formats as supported by curl
+---Does not support @filename style, internal header deletion (e.g: 'Header:')
+---and empty headers 'Header;'

comment_data.body: If we used libcurl we might be able to drop some of these limitations. I personally don't think it would be worth it [to migrate to libcurl], but I thought it was worth mentioning.

comment_data.body:
not trying to golf this, but these 75 lines of test code could be deduped to about 20 lines, and would be much more readable.

```
assert_invalid_header('opts.headers: expected table, got number', 123)
assert_invalid_header(...)
...
```

comment_data.body: This wording is a bit coy. Does this only *add* headers, or can it replace headers (I guess not, if "internal header deletion" means "replace")?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -14,6 +14,11 @@ local M = {}
 ---
 ---Buffer to save the response body to.
 ---@field outbuf? integer
+---
+---Table of custom headers to send with the request.
+---Supports basic key/value header formats and empty headers as supported by curl.
+---Does not support @filename style, internal header deletion (e.g: 'Header:').

comment_data.body:
From `curl(1)`:

> Note that if you should add a custom header that has the same name as one of the internal ones curl would use, your externally set header is used instead of the internal one. This allows you to make even trickier stuff than curl would normally do. You should not replace internally set headers without knowing perfectly well what you are doing. Remove an internal header by giving a replacement without content on the right side of the colon, as in: -H "Host:". If you send the custom header with no-value then its header must be terminated with a semicolon, such as -H "X-Custom-Header;" to send "X-Custom-Header:".

So it can add or replace, but not remove.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -14,6 +14,11 @@ local M = {}
 ---
 ---Buffer to save the response body to.
 ---@field outbuf? integer
+---
+---Table of custom headers to send with the request.
+---Supports basic key/value header formats and empty headers as supported by curl.
+---Does not support @filename style, internal header deletion (e.g: 'Header:').

title: feat(vim.net): custom request() headers
